{{nihongo|'''Wonono Shrine'''|尾野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称 = 尾野神社 | 画像 = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|4|27|N|136|40|37|E}}}} | 所在地 = [[三重県]][[桑名市]]大字東方2194 | 緯度度 = 35| 緯度分 = 4| 緯度秒 = 27 | 経度度 = 136| 経度分 = 40| 経度秒 = 37 | 祭神 = 天押帯日子命 | 神体 = | 社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[県社]] | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = 10月15日 | 神事 = | 地図 = Japan Mie }}
'''尾野神社'''（おのじんじゃ）は、[[三重県]][[桑名市]]白子に[[鎮座]]する神社である。[[延喜式内社]]で旧社格は[[県社]]。
== 由緒 == 創祀は、崇神天皇28年と伝わる。往古は、御陵の上に神殿があった。 永禄10年、織田信長によって焼失。御陵の下に神殿が移る。 明治29年、県社に昇格。明治41年、式内社の立坂神社を合祀<ref>{{Cite web|title=尾野神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>。
== 祭神 == * 祭神：大押帯日子命
== 例祭 == *10月15日
== 関連項目 == *[[伊勢国の式内社一覧]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}}

== 由緒 == 創祀は、[[崇神天皇]]28年と伝わる。往古は、古墳の上に神殿があった。[[永禄]]10年、[[織田信長]]によって焼失。古墳の下に神殿が移る。[[明治]]29年、県社に昇格。明治41年、式内社の立坂神社を合祀<ref>{{Cite web|title=尾野神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>。祭神の大押帯日子命は、[[孝昭天皇]]の皇子。
== 祭神 == * 祭神：大押帯日子命
== 例祭 == *10月15日

'''尾野神社'''（おのじんじゃ）は、[[三重県]][[桑名市]]白子に[[鎮座]]する神社である。[[延喜式内社]]で旧社格は[[県社]]。別名、船着大明神<ref>{{Cite web|title=尾野神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>。
== 由緒 == 創祀は、[[崇神天皇]]28年と伝わる。伊勢国の桑名野代宮の、[[元伊勢]]の比定社でもある。往古は、古墳の上に神殿があった。[[永禄]]10年、[[織田信長]]によって焼失。古墳の下に神殿が移る。[[明治]]29年、県社に昇格。明治41年、式内社の立坂神社を合祀<ref>{{Cite web|title=尾野神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>。祭神の大押帯日子命は、[[孝昭天皇]]の皇子。
== 祭神 == * 祭神：大押帯日子命<ref>{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>
== 例祭 == *10月15日
== 関連項目 == *[[伊勢国の式内社一覧]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:おのしんしや}} [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:桑名市の歴史]] [[Category:桑名市の建築物]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]]

{{nihongo|'''Wonono Shrine'''|尾野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Wonono Shrine'''|尾野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

{{Infobox religious building

| native_name = 尾野神社

{{nihongo|'''Wonono Shrine'''|尾野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

revision.comment: 6 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称 = 尾野神社 | 画像 = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|4|27|N|136|40|37|E}}}} | 所在地 = [[三重県]][[桑名市]]大字東方2194 | 緯度度 = 35| 緯度分 = 4| 緯度秒 = 27 | 経度度 = 136| 経度分 = 40| 経度秒 = 37 | 祭神 = 天押帯日子命 | 神体 = | 社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[県社]] | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = 10月15日 | 神事 = | 地図 = Japan Mie }}
'''尾野神社'''（おのじんじゃ）は、[[三重県]][[桑名市]]白子に[[鎮座]]する神社である。[[延喜式内社]]で旧社格は[[県社]]。別名、船着大明神<ref>{{Cite web|title=尾野神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>。
== 由緒 == 創祀は、[[崇神天皇]]28年と伝わる。[[伊勢国]]の桑名野代宮の、[[元伊勢]]の比定社でもある。往古は、古墳の上に神殿があった。[[永禄]]10年、[[織田信長]]によって焼失。古墳の下に神殿が移る。[[明治]]29年、県社に昇格。明治41年、式内社の立坂神社を合祀<ref>{{Cite web|title=尾野神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>。祭神の大押帯日子命は、[[孝昭天皇]]の皇子。
== 祭神 == * 祭神：大押帯日子命<ref>{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>
== 例祭 == *10月15日
== 関連項目 == *[[伊勢国の式内社一覧]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:おのしんしや}} [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:桑名市の歴史]] [[Category:桑名市の建築物]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]]

'''尾野神社'''（おのじんじゃ）は、[[三重県]][[桑名市]]白子に[[鎮座]]する神社である。[[延喜式内社]]で旧社格は[[県社]]。別名、船着大明神<ref>{{Cite web|title=尾野神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>。
== 由緒 == 創祀は、[[崇神天皇]]28年と伝わる。[[伊勢国]]の桑名野代宮の、[[元伊勢]]の比定社でもある。往古は、古墳の上に神殿があった。[[永禄]]10年、[[織田信長]]によって焼失。古墳の下に神殿が移る。[[明治]]29年、県社に昇格。明治41年、式内社の立坂神社を合祀<ref>{{Cite web|title=尾野神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>。祭神の大押帯日子命は、[[孝昭天皇]]の皇子。
== 祭神 == * 祭神：大押帯日子命<ref>{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>
== 例祭 == *10月15日
== 関連項目 == *[[伊勢国の式内社一覧]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:おのしんしや}} [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:桑名市の歴史]] [[Category:桑名市の建築物]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]]
==End Japanese==

[[JA:尾野神社 (桑名市)]]
{{wikidata link|Q135039448}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
{{translated page|ja|尾野神社 (桑名市)|version=107479325|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]
== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 | 名称 = 尾野神社 | 画像 = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|35|4|27|N|136|40|37|E}}}} | 所在地 = [[三重県]][[桑名市]]大字東方2194 | 緯度度 = 35| 緯度分 = 4| 緯度秒 = 27 | 経度度 = 136| 経度分 = 40| 経度秒 = 37 | 祭神 = 天押帯日子命 | 神体 = | 社格 = [[式内社]]、旧[[県社]] | 創建 = | 本殿 = | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = 10月15日 | 神事 = | 地図 = Japan Mie }}
'''尾野神社'''（おのじんじゃ）は、[[三重県]][[桑名市]]白子に[[鎮座]]する神社である。[[延喜式内社]]で旧社格は[[県社]]。別名、船着大明神<ref>{{Cite web|title=尾野神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>。
== 由緒 == 創祀は、[[崇神天皇]]28年と伝わる。[[伊勢国]]の桑名野代宮の、[[元伊勢]]の比定社でもある。往古は、古墳の上に神殿があった。[[永禄]]10年、[[織田信長]]によって焼失。古墳の下に神殿が移る。[[明治]]29年、県社に昇格。明治41年、式内社の立坂神社を合祀<ref>{{Cite web|title=尾野神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>。祭神の大押帯日子命は、[[孝昭天皇]]の皇子。
== 祭神 == * 祭神：大押帯日子命<ref>{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>
== 例祭 == *10月15日
== 関連項目 == *[[伊勢国の式内社一覧]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}}
==End Japanese== [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:桑名市の歴史]] [[Category:桑名市の建築物]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:元伊勢]] [[Category:三重県の旧県社]]

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
| 名称 = 尾野神社
| 画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=桑名市}}
| 所在地 = {{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|桑名市|Q496472}}大字東方2194
| 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}}
| 祭神 = 天押帯日子命
| 神体 = 
| 社格 =  {{jalink|式内社}}、旧{{jalink|県社}}
| 創建 =

| 別名 =

| 神事 = 
| 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mie|label=尾野神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}
}}
'''尾野神社'''（おのじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|桑名市|Q496472}}白子に{{jalink|鎮座}}する神社である。{{jalink|延喜式内社}}で旧社格は{{jalink|県社}}。別名、船着大明神<ref>{{Cite web|title=尾野神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>。

=== 由緒 ===
創祀は、{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}28年と伝わる。{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}の桑名野代宮の、{{jalink|元伊勢|Q11387775}}の比定社でもある。往古は、古墳の上に神殿があった。{{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}10年、{{jalink|織田信長|Q171411}}によって焼失。古墳の下に神殿が移る。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}29年、県社に昇格。明治41年、式内社の立坂神社を合祀<ref>{{Cite web|title=尾野神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>。祭神の大押帯日子命は、{{jalink|孝昭天皇|Q312841}}の皇子。

=== 祭神 ===
* 祭神：大押帯日子命<ref>{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>

=== 例祭 ===
*10月15日

{{nihongo|'''Wonono Shrine'''|尾野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}}

'''尾野神社'''（おのじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|桑名市|Q496472}}白子に{{jalink|鎮座}}する神社である。{{jalink|延喜式内社}}で旧社格は{{jalink|県社}}。別名、船着大明神<ref>{{Cite web|title=尾野神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>。

=== 由緒 ===
創祀は、{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}28年と伝わる。{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}の桑名野代宮の、{{jalink|元伊勢|Q11387775}}の比定社でもある。往古は、古墳の上に神殿があった。{{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}10年、{{jalink|織田信長|Q171411}}によって焼失。古墳の下に神殿が移る。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}29年、県社に昇格。明治41年、式内社の立坂神社を合祀<ref>{{Cite web|title=尾野神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>。祭神の大押帯日子命は、{{jalink|孝昭天皇|Q312841}}の皇子。

=== 祭神 ===
* 祭神：大押帯日子命<ref>{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>

=== 例祭 ===
*10月15日

'''尾野神社'''（おのじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|三重県|Q128196}}{{jalink|桑名市|Q496472}}白子に{{jalink|鎮座}}する神社である。{{jalink|延喜式内社}}で旧社格は{{jalink|県社}}。別名、船着大明神<ref>{{Cite web|title=尾野神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>。

=== 由緒 ===
創祀は、{{jalink|崇神天皇|Q333530}}28年と伝わる。{{jalink|伊勢国|Q864166}}の桑名野代宮の、{{jalink|元伊勢|Q11387775}}の比定社でもある。往古は、古墳の上に神殿があった。{{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}10年、{{jalink|織田信長|Q171411}}によって焼失。古墳の下に神殿が移る。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}29年、県社に昇格。明治41年、式内社の立坂神社を合祀<ref>{{Cite web|title=尾野神社|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>。祭神の大押帯日子命は、{{jalink|孝昭天皇|Q312841}}の皇子。

=== 祭神 ===
* 祭神：大押帯日子命<ref>{{Cite web|title=三重県神社庁|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>

=== 例祭 ===
*10月15日

=== 関連項目 ===
*{{jalink|伊勢国の式内社一覧|Q11378973}}

=== 脚注 ===

'''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine enshrined in Shirako, {{jalink|Kuwana City|Q496472}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki-listed shrine}} (shikinaisha), and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. It is also known as Funatsuki Daimyōjin.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>

=== History ===
The founding is said to date to the 28th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}. It is also considered a candidate site for the {{jalink|Moto-Ise|Q11387775}} of the Kuwana Nodai-no-miya in {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}. In ancient times, the shrine building stood atop a kofun (ancient burial mound). In the 10th year of {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}}, it was burned down by {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}}, and the shrine building was moved to the base of the kofun. In the 29th year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, it was elevated to the rank of Prefectural Shrine. In Meiji 41 (1908), the Shikinaisha Tachisaka Shrine was amalgamated into it.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref> The enshrined deity, Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto, was a prince of {{jalink|Emperor Kōshō|Q312841}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Enshrined deity: Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Association|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>

=== Annual festival ===
* October 15

{{nihongo|'''Wonono Shrine'''|尾野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

'''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine enshrined in Shirako, {{jalink|Kuwana City|Q496472}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki-listed shrine}} (shikinaisha), and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. It is also known as Funatsuki Daimyōjin.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>

=== History ===
The founding is said to date to the 28th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}. It is also considered a candidate site for the {{jalink|Moto-Ise|Q11387775}} of the Kuwana Nodai-no-miya in {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}. In ancient times, the shrine building stood atop a kofun (ancient burial mound). In the 10th year of {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}}, it was burned down by {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}}, and the shrine building was moved to the base of the kofun. In the 29th year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, it was elevated to the rank of Prefectural Shrine. In Meiji 41 (1908), the Shikinaisha Tachisaka Shrine was amalgamated into it.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref> The enshrined deity, Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto, was a prince of {{jalink|Emperor Kōshō|Q312841}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Enshrined deity: Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Association|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>

=== Annual festival ===
* October 15

'''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine enshrined in Shirako, {{jalink|Kuwana City|Q496472}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki-listed shrine}} (shikinaisha), and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. It is also known as Funatsuki Daimyōjin.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>

=== History ===
The founding is said to date to the 28th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}. It is also considered a candidate site for the {{jalink|Moto-Ise|Q11387775}} of the Kuwana Nodai-no-miya in {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}. In ancient times, the shrine building stood atop a kofun (ancient burial mound). In the 10th year of {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}}, it was burned down by {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}}, and the shrine building was moved to the base of the kofun. In the 29th year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, it was elevated to the rank of Prefectural Shrine. In Meiji 41 (1908), the Shikinaisha Tachisaka Shrine was amalgamated into it.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref> The enshrined deity, Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto, was a prince of {{jalink|Emperor Kōshō|Q312841}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Enshrined deity: Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Association|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>

}}
'''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine enshrined in Shirako, {{jalink|Kuwana City|Q496472}}, {{jalink|Mie Prefecture|Q128196}}. It is an {{jalink|Engishiki-listed shrine}} (shikinaisha), and its former shrine rank was {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}}. It is also known as Funatsuki Daimyōjin.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>

=== History ===
The founding is said to date to the 28th year of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}. It is also considered a candidate site for the {{jalink|Moto-Ise|Q11387775}} of the Kuwana Nodai-no-miya in {{jalink|Ise Province|Q864166}}. In ancient times, the shrine building stood atop a kofun (ancient burial mound). In the 10th year of {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}}, it was burned down by {{jalink|Oda Nobunaga|Q171411}}, and the shrine building was moved to the base of the kofun. In the 29th year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, it was elevated to the rank of Prefectural Shrine. In Meiji 41 (1908), the Shikinaisha Tachisaka Shrine was amalgamated into it.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref> The enshrined deity, Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto, was a prince of {{jalink|Emperor Kōshō|Q312841}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Enshrined deity: Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Association|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>

=== Annual festival ===
* October 15

'''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine enshrined in Shirako, {{ill|lt=Kuwana City|Q496472|WD=Q496472}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is an {{ill||lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|UNKNOWN}} (shikinaisha), and its former shrine rank was {{ill||lt=Prefectural Shrine|UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as Funatsuki Daimyōjin.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>

=== History ===
The founding is said to date to the 28th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}. It is also considered a candidate site for the {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775}} of the Kuwana Nodai-no-miya in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}. In ancient times, the shrine building stood atop a kofun (ancient burial mound). In the 10th year of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}}, it was burned down by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}, and the shrine building was moved to the base of the kofun. In the 29th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}, it was elevated to the rank of Prefectural Shrine. In Meiji 41 (1908), the Shikinaisha Tachisaka Shrine was amalgamated into it.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref> The enshrined deity, Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Enshrined deity: Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Association|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>

=== Annual festival ===
* October 15

'''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine enshrined in Shirako, {{ill|lt=Kuwana City|Q496472|WD=Q496472}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|UNKNOWN}} (shikinaisha), and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as Funatsuki Daimyōjin.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>

=== History ===
The founding is said to date to the 28th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}. It is also considered a candidate site for the {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775}} of the Kuwana Nodai-no-miya in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}. In ancient times, the shrine building stood atop a kofun (ancient burial mound). In the 10th year of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}}, it was burned down by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}, and the shrine building was moved to the base of the kofun. In the 29th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}, it was elevated to the rank of Prefectural Shrine. In Meiji 41 (1908), the Shikinaisha Tachisaka Shrine was amalgamated into it.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref> The enshrined deity, Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Enshrined deity: Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Association|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>

=== Annual festival ===
* October 15

=== Related items ===
* {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|Q11378973|WD=Q11378973}}

In the 10th year of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}}, it was burned down by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}, and the shrine building was moved to the base of the kofun. In the 29th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}, it was elevated to the rank of Prefectural Shrine. In Meiji 41 (1908), the Shikinaisha Tachisaka Shrine was amalgamated into it.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref> The enshrined deity, Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Enshrined deity: Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Association|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>

'''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine enshrined in Shirako, {{ill|lt=Kuwana City|Q496472|WD=Q496472}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (shikinaisha), and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as Funatsuki Daimyōjin.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>

=== History ===
The founding is said to date to the 28th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}. It is also considered a candidate site for the {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775}} of the Kuwana Nodai-no-miya in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}. In ancient times, the shrine building stood atop a kofun (ancient burial mound). In the 10th year of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}}, it was burned down by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}, and the shrine building was moved to the base of the kofun. In the 29th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}, it was elevated to the rank of Prefectural Shrine. In Meiji 41 (1908), the Shikinaisha Tachisaka Shrine was amalgamated into it.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref> The enshrined deity, Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Enshrined deity: Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Association|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>

=== Annual festival ===
* October 15

=== Related items ===
* {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|Q11378973|WD=Q11378973}}

'''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine enshrined in Shirako, {{ill|lt=Kuwana City|Q496472|WD=Q496472}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (shikinaisha), and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as Funatsuki Daimyōjin.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>

=== History ===
The founding is said to date to the 28th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}. It is also considered a candidate site for the {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775}} of the Kuwana Nodai-no-miya in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166}}. In ancient times, the shrine building stood atop a kofun (ancient burial mound). In the 10th year of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}}, it was burned down by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}, and the shrine building was moved to the base of the kofun. In the 29th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}, it was elevated to the rank of Prefectural Shrine. In Meiji 41 (1908), the Shikinaisha Tachisaka Shrine was amalgamated into it.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref> The enshrined deity, Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Enshrined deity: Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Association|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>

=== Annual festival ===
* October 15

{{nihongo|'''Wonono Shrine'''|尾野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

'''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine enshrined in Shirako, {{ill|lt=Kuwana City|Q496472|WD=Q496472|1=Kuwana, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (shikinaisha), and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as Funatsuki Daimyōjin.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>

=== History ===
The founding is said to date to the 28th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}. It is also considered a candidate site for the {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}} of the Kuwana Nodai-no-miya in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}. In ancient times, the shrine building stood atop a kofun (ancient burial mound). In the 10th year of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}}, it was burned down by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and the shrine building was moved to the base of the kofun. In the 29th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was elevated to the rank of Prefectural Shrine. In Meiji 41 (1908), the Shikinaisha Tachisaka Shrine was amalgamated into it.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref> The enshrined deity, Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Enshrined deity: Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Association|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>

=== Annual festival ===
* October 15

'''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine enshrined in Shirako, {{ill|lt=Kuwana City|Q496472|WD=Q496472|1=Kuwana, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (shikinaisha), and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as Funatsuki Daimyōjin.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>

=== History ===
The founding is said to date to the 28th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}. It is also considered a candidate site for the {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}} of the Kuwana Nodai-no-miya in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}. In ancient times, the shrine building stood atop a kofun (ancient burial mound). In the 10th year of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}}, it was burned down by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and the shrine building was moved to the base of the kofun. In the 29th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was elevated to the rank of Prefectural Shrine. In Meiji 41 (1908), the Shikinaisha Tachisaka Shrine was amalgamated into it.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref> The enshrined deity, Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Enshrined deity: Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Association|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>

=== Annual festival ===
* October 15

=== History ===
The founding is said to date to the 28th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}. It is also considered a candidate site for the {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}} of the Kuwana Nodai-no-miya in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}. In ancient times, the shrine building stood atop a kofun (ancient burial mound). In the 10th year of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}}, it was burned down by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and the shrine building was moved to the base of the kofun. In the 29th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was elevated to the rank of Prefectural Shrine. In Meiji 41 (1908), the Shikinaisha Tachisaka Shrine was amalgamated into it.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref> The enshrined deity, Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Enshrined deity: Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Association|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>

=== Annual festival ===
* October 15

'''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine enshrined in Shirako, {{ill|lt=Kuwana City|Q496472|WD=Q496472|1=Kuwana, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (shikinaisha), and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as Funatsuki Daimyōjin.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>

=== History ===
The founding is said to date to the 28th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}. It is also considered a candidate site for the {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}} of the Kuwana Nodai-no-miya in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}. In ancient times, the shrine building stood atop a kofun (ancient burial mound). In the 10th year of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}}, it was burned down by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and the shrine building was moved to the base of the kofun. In the 29th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was elevated to the rank of Prefectural Shrine. In Meiji 41 (1908), the Shikinaisha Tachisaka Shrine was amalgamated into it.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref> The enshrined deity, Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Enshrined deity: Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Association|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>

=== Annual festival ===
* October 15

'''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine enshrined in Shirako, {{ill|lt=Kuwana City|Q496472|WD=Q496472|1=Kuwana, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (shikinaisha), and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as Funatsuki Daimyōjin.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>

=== History ===
The founding is said to date to the 28th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}. It is also considered a candidate site for the {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}} of the Kuwana Nodai-no-miya in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}. In ancient times, the shrine building stood atop a kofun (ancient burial mound). In the 10th year of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}}, it was burned down by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and the shrine building was moved to the base of the kofun. In the 29th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was elevated to the rank of Prefectural Shrine. In Meiji 41 (1908), the Shikinaisha Tachisaka Shrine was amalgamated into it.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref> The enshrined deity, Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Enshrined deity: Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Association|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>

=== Annual festival ===
* October 15

'''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine enshrined in Shirako, {{ill|lt=Kuwana City|Q496472|WD=Q496472|1=Kuwana, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (shikinaisha), and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as Funatsuki Daimyōjin.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>

=== History ===
The founding is said to date to the 28th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}. It is also considered a candidate site for the {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}} of the Kuwana Nodai-no-miya in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}. In ancient times, the shrine building stood atop a kofun (ancient burial mound). In the 10th year of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}}, it was burned down by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and the shrine building was moved to the base of the kofun. In the 29th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was elevated to the rank of Prefectural Shrine. In Meiji 41 (1908), the Shikinaisha Tachisaka Shrine was amalgamated into it.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref> The enshrined deity, Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Enshrined deity: Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Association|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>

=== Annual festival ===
* October 15

{{nihongo|'''Wonono Shrine'''|尾野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

'''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine enshrined in Shirako, {{ill|lt=Kuwana City|Q496472|WD=Q496472|1=Kuwana, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (shikinaisha), and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as Funatsuki Daimyōjin.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>

=== History ===
The founding is said to date to the 28th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}. It is also considered a candidate site for the {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}} of the Kuwana Nodai-no-miya in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}. In ancient times, the shrine building stood atop a kofun (ancient burial mound). In the 10th year of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}}, it was burned down by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and the shrine building was moved to the base of the kofun. In the 29th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was elevated to the rank of Prefectural Shrine. In Meiji 41 (1908), the Shikinaisha Tachisaka Shrine was amalgamated into it.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref> The enshrined deity, Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Enshrined deity: Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Association|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>

=== Annual festival ===
* October 15

{{nihongo|'''Wonono Shrine'''|尾野神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]].

'''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine enshrined in Shirako, {{ill|lt=Kuwana City|Q496472|WD=Q496472|1=Kuwana, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (shikinaisha), and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as Funatsuki Daimyōjin.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>

=== History ===
The founding is said to date to the 28th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}. It is also considered a candidate site for the {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}} of the Kuwana Nodai-no-miya in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}. In ancient times, the shrine building stood atop a kofun (ancient burial mound). In the 10th year of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}}, it was burned down by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and the shrine building was moved to the base of the kofun. In the 29th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was elevated to the rank of Prefectural Shrine. In Meiji 41 (1908), the Shikinaisha Tachisaka Shrine was amalgamated into it.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref> The enshrined deity, Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Enshrined deity: Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Association|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>

=== Annual festival ===
* October 15

=== Related items ===
* {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|Q11378973|WD=Q11378973|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province}}

'''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine enshrined in Shirako, {{ill|lt=Kuwana City|Q496472|WD=Q496472|1=Kuwana, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (shikinaisha), and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as Funatsuki Daimyōjin.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>

=== History ===
The founding is said to date to the 28th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}. It is also considered a candidate site for the {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}} of the Kuwana Nodai-no-miya in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}. In ancient times, the shrine building stood atop a kofun (ancient burial mound). In the 10th year of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}}, it was burned down by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and the shrine building was moved to the base of the kofun. In the 29th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was elevated to the rank of Prefectural Shrine. In Meiji 41 (1908), the Shikinaisha Tachisaka Shrine was amalgamated into it.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref> The enshrined deity, Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Enshrined deity: Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Association|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>

'''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine enshrined in Shirako, {{ill|lt=Kuwana City|Q496472|WD=Q496472|1=Kuwana, Mie}}, {{ill|lt=Mie Prefecture|Q128196|WD=Q128196|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki-listed shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (shikinaisha), and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is also known as Funatsuki Daimyōjin.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-04}}</ref>

=== History ===
The founding is said to date to the 28th year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}. It is also considered a candidate site for the {{ill|lt=Moto-Ise|Q11387775|WD=Q11387775|1=Moto-Ise Shrines}} of the Kuwana Nodai-no-miya in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}. In ancient times, the shrine building stood atop a kofun (ancient burial mound). In the 10th year of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}}, it was burned down by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and the shrine building was moved to the base of the kofun. In the 29th year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was elevated to the rank of Prefectural Shrine. In Meiji 41 (1908), the Shikinaisha Tachisaka Shrine was amalgamated into it.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Ono Shrine|url=http://engishiki.org/ise/bun/is081307-01.html|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref> The enshrined deity, Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto, was a prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Enshrined deity: Ōoshiobi Hikonomikoto<ref>{{Cite web|title=Mie Prefecture Shrine Association|url=http://kyoka.mie-jinjacho.or.jp/shrine/%e5%b0%be%e9%87%8e%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/|access-date=2025-12-4}}</ref>

=== Annual festival ===
* October 15
